title: cryptocarya globosa
intro: Cryptocarya globosa, also known as the Ceylon ironwood or native ebony, is a species of evergreen tree native to Sri Lanka. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat respiratory issues, fever, and digestive problems. The plant's bark and leaves contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and alleviating symptoms of colds and flu. In traditional medicine, the tree's bark, leaves, and roots are prepared in various herbal preparations, such as decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties.
